projects.name: ILC1/7 VFD clock large Soviet vacuum fluorescent display DIY
projects.blurb: A clock project for the large VFD tubes ILC1-1/7 model made in the Soviet era. It uses a preprogrammed microcontroller & standard parts
projects.html_story:
A clock project for the large VFD tubes ILC1-1/7 model made in the Soviet era. It uses a preprogrammed microcontroller & standard parts

A clock project for the large VFD tubes ILC1-1/7 model made in the Soviet era. It uses a preprogrammed microcontroller & standard parts

The idea for this project began with a request from a kit builder who owned these vintage VFD tubes. These are very large vacuum fluorescent display tubes from the Soviet era. Here is the link to a partial description of the specifications of these tubes:Huge 7-Segment VFD ILC1-1/7 (ИЛЦ1-1/7).

After examining the specifications and studying the tubes, we developed a prototype PC board and driver and wrote some firmware to create the clock project. The kit we developed is DIY for anyone who can solder competently and identify some basic electronic components. Everything is standard sized with no surface mount parts so a novice solderer can do it. The chips including the preprogrammed microcontroller are set in sockets for easy replacement, and there are very few other components in the design.

The prototype PCBs are the "barebones" design which includes trace on the board only, no solder mask or silkscreen. The production run will include full coverage in solder mask and silkscreen, making it easy to assemble for a skilled kit builder.

The design is modular, so that seconds can be added. The board has a port for GPS or Wi-Fi module input as well.

Risks and challenges

The biggest challenge to this project was supplying the proper power for these large tubes, which draw about 200ma each on the filament only! We designed a robust "overkill" power supply for this, to drive the tubes and power the drivers.

projects.ai_intro_en: A DIY clock kit for the large Soviet-era VFD tubes (ILC1-1/7). It uses a preprogrammed microcontroller and standard through-hole parts, with no surface-mount components, making it accessible to novice solderers. The modular design supports adding seconds, GPS, or Wi-Fi, and production PCBs will feature solder mask and silkscreen for easy assembly.
ai_highlights_en: Vintage Soviet VFD tube clock kit with large 7-segment displays
Easy DIY assembly using standard parts and socketed chips
Modular design with optional seconds, GPS, and Wi-Fi modules
ai_specs_en: Robust design for high filament current
Tier: Deluxe kit with enclosure color PCB
projects.ai_risks_en: The main challenge is powering the large VFD tubes, which draw about 200mA each on the filament. The project mitigates this with a robust, over-engineered power supply designed specifically for these demanding tubes.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: Electronics has been my interest since the days of the 1960's space race, and in recent years I became interested in sharing my project development to fund new designs, and this platform has been excellent for doing so.
projects.ai_description_en:
This is a DIY clock kit designed around the rare ILC1-7 vacuum fluorescent display tubes from the Soviet era. These are large-format 7-segment VFD tubes that draw approximately 200mA each just for the filament current, making them notoriously difficult to power properly. The project provides everything a skilled kit builder needs to bring these vintage displays back to life as a working timepiece, combining historical Soviet hardware with a modern pre-programmed microcontroller driver board.

The kit is built for competent hobbyists, using only standard through-hole components with no surface-mount parts required. The microcontroller comes pre-programmed and socketed for easy replacement, significantly reducing the assembly challenge. The power supply was specifically engineered from scratch—describing it as "overkill" because safely driving these power-hungry vintage tubes at the proper voltage and current was the project's primary engineering challenge. The modular design includes expansion ports for adding a seconds display, GPS module, or Wi-Fi connectivity, allowing builders to choose their preferred time source and level of complexity.

For collectors and electronics enthusiasts, this kit represents an accessible entry point into Soviet-era VFD technology. The large format of the ILC1-7 tubes makes them visually striking display components, and the chance to build a functional clock from genuine Cold War-era hardware carries intrinsic appeal. The DIY nature of the project—requiring only basic soldering skills and component identification—lowers the barrier to entry compared to other vintage VFD projects that demand specialist equipment or surface-mount expertise. The production PCBs will feature full solder mask and silkscreen coverage, further simplifying the assembly process for backers.

Whether completed as a standalone timepiece or expanded with GPS synchronization for automatic time accuracy, this kit offers a hands-on way to preserve and utilize a genuinely historical display technology. The modular hardware design ensures the project stays flexible, while the thoughtful integration of sockets and standard components makes troubleshooting and repair straightforward. For those seeking a unique blend of vintage Soviet engineering and modern DIY electronics, this kit builds something that's both technically rewarding and conversation-starting.

projects.ai_intro_en: The AirTrek SmartBag is a 3-in-1 backpack, trolley, and smart compression travel bag built for modern mobility. It features auto-compression technology, 60L high capacity, and smart organization as your all-in-one travel companion.
ai_highlights_en: 3-in-1 design: backpack, trolley, and smart compression bag
Auto-compression technology for space-saving travel
60L high capacity with smart organization system
projects.ai_risks_en: Standard crowdfunding risks apply including potential delivery delays, product changes from prototype to final version, and the possibility that the project may not reach its funding goal. Specific risks are not detailed on this page.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: LD BAG - Creator of the AirTrek SmartBag travel solution.
projects.ai_description_en:
The AirTrek SmartBag is a new breed of travel gear from LD BAG, engineered to replace multiple bags with a single, shape-shifting solution. It functions as a 2-in-1 trolley and backpack, then adds a smart compression layer to earn its “3-in-1” identity. Whether you need a rolling suitcase for terminal corridors, a comfortable backpack for cobblestone streets, or a bulk‑reduced compression bag that packs its own contents down, the AirTrek SmartBag transforms to match the moment. With a generous 60 L high-capacity interior, it carries everything for extended trips without forcing you to check bags or wrestle with separate carriers.

At its core is an intelligent auto-compression system that evenly presses clothing and soft goods to reclaim valuable space, so you can fit more without exceeding carry‑on limits. Smart organization is built in: dedicated compartments separate shoes, tech, and travel essentials, keeping items instantly accessible while protecting delicate gear. Switching between backpack and trolley modes is designed to be fast and intuitive, letting you pull the bag for smooth mobility on flat surfaces or shoulder it confidently when the terrain turns rugged.

Why it matters: modern travel rarely follows a single path, and fumbling with multiple bags kills momentum. The AirTrek SmartBag delivers genuine versatility for the way people actually move — through airports, onto trains, up stairwells, and into campsites — without compromise. It’s the all-in-one travel companion for backpackers, digital nomads, and outdoor adventurers who refuse to be defined by a single mode of movement. From a project built on real mobility challenges, this smart compression travel bag finally unifies protection, capacity, and adaptability into one streamlined design.

projects.name: PFM Module | Zafira B & Astra H module to fold mirrors
projects.blurb: Custom build micro-controller for automatically folding and unfolding power folding mirrors on Opel/Vauxhall Astra H/Zafira B upon closing/opening the car with car's remote (key fob).
projects.html_story: Project storyCreatormirontomore infocontactCategoryTransportationTagscarsBacking informationCrowdfunding riskslearn moreSince I bought my Zafira in 2013, I dreamed of a modification, that would enable to automatically fold/unfold the mirrors via the remote key. Finally, after I tinkered with the driver door switch in December 2018, I decided to create a module that would enable me to fold the mirrors upon locking and unfold upon unlocking the car (like you get it with many other car brands as standard).Upon reverse engineering the driver door switch I discovered that the mirrors are operated via a switching relay controlled by a micro controller. Upon pressing the button, the relay is engaged, sending voltage to mirror motors for a defined period of time. The Opel implementation is really simple and there is nothing fancy like detecting the position of the mirrors or stop switches. The controller just cycles through unfolding/folding upon each press of a button.To mimic this behavior I’ve decided to use similar approach with a module containing a micro-controller and relays. The controller would detect lock/unlock signals from door lock motor wires and control two relays – one would disconnect mirror motors from driver door switch unit connecting them to the module during the operation of folding/unfolding (and connect them back to driver door switch unit afterwards) and the second relay would function the same way as the relay in the driver door switch unit – fold and unfold the mirrors by sending 12V to mirror motors for a defined time period.As I am very pleased of how well the Power Fold Mirrors aka PFM module turned out, I decided to produce a small batch of 20 modules for other Zafira B owners to enjoy. And since Zafira B and Astra H have so much in common, the PFM module can be fitted to Astra H as well as the electrical side of mirrors is 100% identical to Zafira B.Will ship to Europe and UK for aflat fee of 10€(not includedin the pledge amount).

projects.ai_intro_en: The PFM Module is a custom micro-controller that enables automatic folding and unfolding of power folding mirrors on Opel/Vauxhall Astra H and Zafira B when locking or unlocking the car via the remote key fob. It detects lock/unlock signals from door lock motor wires and controls relays to send 12V to mirror motors for a defined period, mimicking factory behavior.
ai_highlights_en: Automatically folds/unfolds mirrors via car remote key fob
Compatible with both Opel Zafira B and Astra H models
Custom micro-controller with relay-based control system
projects.ai_risks_en: The creator needs a batch of 20 modules to make manufacturing profitable. If all needed parts cannot be sourced after the campaign ends, the project will be cancelled and 100% refunds issued. The project is only 10% funded with 56 days remaining.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: Miroslav Sturmankin (mironto), a car enthusiast who reverse-engineered the driver door switch to create the PFM module.
projects.ai_description_en:
Upgrade your driving experience with the Power Fold Mirrors (PFM) module, a custom-engineered upgrade for your Opel Zafira B or Astra H. The PFM is an electronic control module that automatically folds and unfolds your side mirrors using your car's existing remote key. No more manually reaching out to adjust or fold your mirrors in tight parking spaces; the PFM seamlessly integrates with your vehicle to deliver the convenience of a premium factory feature.

At the core of the PFM is a micro-controller paired with a dual-relay system, designed after reverse-engineering the Opel driver door switch. When you lock or unlock the car, the module detects the signal from the door lock motor wires. It temporarily disengages the mirror motors from the driver door switch, sends the precise 12V voltage needed to fold or unfold the mirrors, and then safely reconnects them to the original switch. This unique relay logic mimics the factory cycling behavior perfectly, alternating between folding and unfolding with each press.

The PFM module solves the frustration of outdated manual mirror systems, bringing expected modern convenience to everyday drivers. By protecting mirrors from tight parking spaces and adding a premium touch to your daily drive, it bridges the gap between older automotive designs and current comfort standards. Furthermore, since the electrical systems of the Zafira B and Astra H mirrors are identical, the PFM module fits both models flawlessly without requiring complex position sensors or stop switches.

This sophisticated micro-controller upgrade is being produced in a small, limited batch of just 20 units, ensuring high quality and exclusivity. Shipping to Europe and the UK is available for a flat fee of 10€, allowing you to equipt your Opel with state-of-the-art remote mirror folding quickly and easily.

projects.name: Securidoor
projects.blurb:
The world’s first AI-powered surveillance module for door and window

Transform your door frame or window into a smart first line of defense. Designed to be discreet yet remarkably effective, this standalone module monitors your entrance 24/7.

Unlike traditional cameras that bombard you with unnecessary notifications, our system uses our AlwaysSafe© Artificial Intelligence to filter out the everyday and retain only what's essential. With each detected movement, the module automatically records a photo and a short video clip.

The device's true strength lies in its analytical capabilities: Our AlwaysSafe© AI is trained to recognize specific hostile behaviors. Whether it's an attempt to pick your lock, banging on the door, or acts of vandalism, the system immediately identifies the threat.

In the event of a confirmed threat, you receive an instant alert on your mobile phone, along with video evidence, allowing you to react before the intrusion even occurs.

projects.ai_description_en:
Securidoor is a wireless, AI-powered surveillance module designed to elevate home safety and security within the smart home ecosystem. Addressing the growing demand for intelligent, connected residential protection, Securidoor leverages artificial intelligence to deliver advanced monitoring capabilities without the complexity or cost of traditional security systems. Positioned in the Home category and tagged across safety, security, smart home, and wireless themes, the product offers a streamlined approach to keeping households secure through modern technology.

The current campaign on Indiegogo, created by team Securima, provides backers with three pledge tiers structured for flexibility and value. The Securidoor x1 single unit is offered at an early bird price of €59, down from a future regular price of €99. For multi-unit needs, the Securidoor x2 bundle is available for €110 versus a regular €198, and the Securidoor x3 tier is priced at €151 compared to €297. All tiers include the AI-powered surveillance module with an estimated fulfillment date of July 2026. Additionally, a monthly payment option is available, starting from €29.50 per month for a single unit, making the product accessible across a range of budgets.

The combination of wireless design, AI-driven surveillance, and smart home compatibility positions Securidoor as a compelling option in the crowded security product space. The significant discount on early bird pricing up to approximately 40% below regular retail, along with multi-unit bundles and low monthly payment options, enhances the value proposition for backers. For those seeking a straightforward upgrade to home security through intelligent monitoring, Securidoor presents an accessible entry point into AI-powered protection.

projects.name: CCBOT Indoor: Patented Modular Robot Platform
projects.blurb: A patent-backed Python and ROS-ready robot with phone control, live camera feed, ToF sensor, and modular hardware.
projects.html_story:
All or nothing.This project will only be funded if it reaches its goal by Tue, July 28 2026 2:31 PM EDT.

A patent-backed Python and ROS-ready robot with phone control, live camera feed, ToF sensor, and modular hardware.

A patent-backed Python and ROS-ready robot with phone control, live camera feed, ToF sensor, and modular hardware.

All or nothing.This project will only be funded if it reaches its goal by Tue, July 28 2026 2:31 PM EDT.

CCBOT Indoor: Patented Modular Robot Platform

Environmental commitments

CCBOT Indoor: Patented Modular Robot Platform

CCBOT Indoor is a small patent-backed modular indoor robot platform designed for robotics learning, research, indoor inspection, Python development, and ROS-ready experimentation.

Single-take demo of the current CCBOT Indoor prototype.

The video shows the actual unit being controlled by phone, moving indoors, showing live camera support, basic autonomous driving test mode, and displaying ToF distance sensing.

Phone control interface operating the actual indoor prototype in real time.

Live camera support shown on the phone interface during prototype testing.

Distance readout and manual drive controls used during the indoor demo.

Current Working Prototype

The current CCBOT Indoor prototype integrates a compact wheeled robot body, smartphone control, live camera support, ToF distance sensing, onboard electronics, motor control, and battery power.

The prototype shown here is the actual unit used in the supplementary demo video.

Current assembled CCBOT Indoor prototype - front view.

Top view showing the compact indoor robot body.

Current assembled CCBOT Indoor prototype - side view.

Current assembled CCBOT Indoor prototype - rear view.

Hardware and Software Integration

CCBOT Indoor is not just a remote-control toy. The current prototype combines physical robot hardware with a phone-based control interface, live video support, and sensor feedback.

Current prototype with the top cover removed, showing the internal electronics, battery, wiring, and wheeled chassis used in the working demo unit.

Camera module used for live video streaming.ToF sensor used for distance sensing and basic obstacle detection experiments.Internal electronics layout with battery, motor control hardware, sensor wiring, and camera connection.

Phone interface showing live camera, distance readout, and manual drive controls.Hardware configuration of the current prototype: robot body, camera, ToF sensor, motor control, battery, and onboard electronics.

What Backers Receive

The Founder Batch reward is the indoor wheeled CCBOT Indoor robot only.

Each Founder Batch robot is planned to include:

- 1 assembled CCBOT Indoor Developer Robot- Modular robotics body- Indoor wheeled drive platform- Smartphone control access- Live camera video support- ToF distance sensing- Basic obstacle detection experiments- Python development direction- ROS-ready development direction- Founder updates- Early feedback channel

What Is Not Included

This first Founder Batch does not include:

- Outdoor utility robot platform- Track-based mobility- Water-surface mobility- Snow, lawn, trailer, or heavy-duty modules- Fully basic autonomous driving test mode navigation- Future CCBOT roadmap modes

Those ideas belong to the broader CCBOT platform direction. They are not included in this first indoor robot reward.

CAD Design and Chassis Development

Before assembling the current prototype, we developed the indoor robot chassis in CAD. The design work helped us plan the robot body, removable top cover, internal electronics space, front hardware opening, side access openings, and the general layout for a compact indoor wheeled robot.

CAD design screenshot showing the indoor robot chassis, removable top cover, internal hardware compartments, and front/side module openings during development.

This CAD image shows the development stage before the current physical prototype was assembled.

Patent-Backed Platform Background

CCBOT Indoor is connected to the broader CCBOT modular robotics platform. CCBOT's modular robotic vehicle technology is supported by U.S. Patent No. 12,539,598 B1, issued February 3, 2026.For this Kickstarter campaign, the Founder Batch reward is the indoor wheeled CCBOT Indoor robot shown in the prototype video and photos. The patent image below is included as technical background for the platform architecture behind CCBOT.

For this Kickstarter campaign, the Founder Batch reward is the indoor wheeled CCBOT Indoor robot shown in the prototype video and photos. The patent image below is included as technical background for the platform architecture behind CCBOT.

Patent drawing showing the core chassis and internal modular layout concept behind the CCBOT platform.

Why Kickstarter?

We are launching this campaign to build a small Founder Batch of 10-15 units and get real feedback from early users before scaling.

Funds will help cover:

- Robot base components- Cameras and sensing hardware- 3D-printed body parts- Electronics and wiring- Assembly and testing- Packaging- Backup parts and repairs- Kickstarter and payment processing fees

Keeping the first batch small allows us to stay hands-on, test each unit carefully, and support early backers directly.

Risks and Challenges

CCBOT Indoor is a small Founder Batch hardware project, and we are keeping the first run intentionally limited to 10-15 units so we can manage quality, assembly, testing, and support closely.

The main challenge is moving from a working prototype to a small batch of reliable units. The current prototype demonstrates indoor movement, smartphone control, live camera video support, basic autonomous driving test mode, and ToF distance sensing. The remaining work is focused on repeatable assembly, testing, documentation, packaging, and small mechanical refinements before shipment.

Because the robot uses a modular design and 3D-printed body components, small mechanical improvements can be made quickly. If a printed part needs adjustment, we can revise and reprint it instead of relying on expensive tooling.

Some components, such as cameras, ToF distance sensors, robot base parts, batteries, motor control hardware, or electronics, may have minor supply or pricing changes. To reduce this risk, we plan to use widely available components where practical and keep extra parts on hand for testing and replacement.

Each CCBOT Indoor robot will be assembled and tested before shipping. We plan to check basic movement, smartphone control, live camera video support, ToF distance sensing, and basic obstacle detection behavior before sending units to backers.

CCBOT Indoor is designed as an early developer robot platform for learning, research, indoor inspection, Python development, and ROS-ready experimentation. Some software examples, documentation, and navigation experiments may continue improving after the Founder Batch ships.

Future outdoor, track-based, water-surface, and other CCBOT platform modes are not included in this first CCBOT Indoor Founder Batch.

Overall, we believe the risks are manageable because this is a small batch, the design is modular, many parts can be replaced or reprinted, and we can work directly with early backers to improve the platform.

Future Software Experiments

We have also developed an experimental CCBOT-based game product calledMow My Worldas part of our broader software exploration. It is on Steam around 7/15/2026. It is not included as a guaranteed reward in this Founder Batch. The first Kickstarter reward is focused on the indoor wheeled robot hardware, phone control, live camera support, and ToF distance sensing.

Risks and challenges

CCBOT Indoor is a small Founder Batch hardware project, and we are keeping the first run intentionally limited to 10–15 units so we can manage quality, assembly, and support closely.

The main challenge is moving from a working prototype to a small batch of reliable units. Since the robot uses a modular design and 3D-printed body components, small mechanical improvements can be made quickly. If a printed part needs adjustment, we can revise and reprint it instead of relying on expensive tooling.

Some components, such as cameras, ultrasonic sensors, robot base parts, batteries, or electronics, may have minor supply or pricing changes. To reduce this risk, we plan to use widely available components and keep extra parts on hand for testing and replacement.

Each CCBOT Indoor robot will be assembled and tested before shipping. We plan to check basic movement, smartphone control, live camera video support, and ultrasonic / sonar distance sensing before sending units to backers.

CCBOT Indoor is designed as an early developer robot platform for learning, research, indoor inspection, Python development, and ROS-ready experimentation. Some software examples, documentation, and navigation experiments may continue improving after the Founder Batch ships.

Future track-based and water-surface mobility modules are part of the broader CCBOT platform roadmap. They are not included in this first CCBOT Indoor Founder Batch.

Overall, we believe the risks are manageable because this is a small batch, the design is modular, many parts can be replaced or reprinted, and we can work directly with early backers to improve the platform.

Environmental commitments

Long-lasting design

CCBOT Indoor is designed as a modular robot platform rather than a disposable toy. The body, sensor mounts, electronics, battery, and internal components are designed to be serviceable, replaceable, and upgradeable where practical.

Reusability and recyclability

The robot uses modular parts that can be removed, repaired, replaced, or upgraded. Where practical, we will use reusable or recyclable packaging materials and avoid unnecessary single-use plastics.

Sustainable materials

We plan to use 3D-printed body components where appropriate, which allows small-batch production with reduced tooling waste. When possible, we will use commonly available materials such as PETG and design parts to be replaceable rather than discarded.

Environmentally friendly factories

For this first Founder Batch, we do not plan to use large-scale mass production. We plan to use small-batch 3D printing and hands-on assembly where appropriate, which helps reduce tooling waste, avoid unnecessary overproduction, and keep inventory limited to actual backer demand. If we work with outside suppliers, we will consider component availability, quality, repairability, and responsible small-batch production practices.

Sustainable Distribution

We plan to keep fulfillment small and direct for the Founder Batch. Units will be packed in compact packaging where practical, with reduced excess material and limited unnecessary plastic. Because the first batch is only 10-15 units, we can avoid overproduction and keep shipping volume limited to actual backer demand.

projects.ai_intro_en: CCBOT Indoor is a patent-backed modular indoor robot platform designed for robotics learning, research, and Python/ROS development. It features smartphone control, live camera feed, ToF distance sensing, and a compact wheeled chassis. The current working prototype demonstrates indoor movement, phone control, and basic autonomous driving test mode.
ai_highlights_en: Patent-backed modular robotics platform with U.S. Patent No. 12,539,598 B1
Python and ROS-ready for robotics learning, research, and development
Smartphone control with live camera feed and ToF distance sensing
projects.ai_risks_en: The main challenge is moving from a working prototype to a small batch of reliable units. Remaining work includes repeatable assembly, testing, documentation, packaging, and small mechanical refinements before shipment. The first batch is limited to 10-15 units.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: Founder of CCBOT LLC, building modular robotic platforms for education, inspection, and future multi-mode mobility. Inventor of patented modular robotic vehicle technology and developer of the CCBOT Indoor / outdoor robot platform.
projects.ai_description_en:
CCBOT Indoor is a patent-backed, modular robot platform designed for indoor robotics learning, research, and inspection. This compact wheeled robot combines smartphone control, live camera video streaming, and ToF (Time of Flight) distance sensing in a ready-to-use developer unit. Unlike remote-control toys, CCBOT Indoor is built around Python and ROS-ready experimentation, giving developers and researchers a tangible platform for coding real robotics applications. The entire system integrates onboard electronics, motor control, battery power, and a phone-based control interface that displays live camera feeds and real-time sensor data.

At its core, CCBOT Indoor delivers practical indoor functionality. The prototype demonstrates phone-controlled movement, live camera support, basic autonomous driving test mode, and ultrasonic/sonar distance sensing for obstacle detection experiments. Each unit features a modular robotics body with an indoor wheeled drive platform, allowing developers to interact with sensor feedback in real time. The platform is designed to run Python scripts and support ROS (Robot Operating System) development paths, making it suitable for both educational exploration and advanced robotics research in home or lab environments.

What makes CCBOT Indoor stand out is its modular, patent-backed architecture and the team's commitment to iterative improvement. Using 3D-printed body components means mechanical adjustments can be made quickly without expensive tooling—if a part needs refinement, it can be revised and reprinted. As a limited Founder Batch project capped at 10–15 units, the team focuses on close assembly, testing, and direct backer support. While future modules like track-based and water-surface mobility are planned for the broader CCBOT platform, this first batch delivers the indoor wheeled robot only, assembled and tested to verify movement, smartphone control, and live video support before shipping.

projects.name: Volatco
projects.blurb: A powerful, elegant, and energy-efficient multicomputer for when we replace AI.
projects.hw_reason: Multicomputer hardware
projects.ai_intro_en: Volatco is an ultra-compact, Forth-native multicomputer for real-time control without cloud dependence. Built around 2 x GA144 chips with 288 independent F18A processing nodes, it targets robotics, edge AI, and low-latency control systems. The platform emphasizes deterministic response, low-power event-driven computation, and transparent system design.
ai_highlights_en: 288 independent F18A processing nodes for massively parallel computation
Ultra-low power: ~13uW suspended, ~972mW typical all-nodes-running
Forth-native with no OS overhead for deterministic real-time control
ai_specs_en: Up to 96B primitive ops/s per chip
projects.ai_risks_en: Volatco is a specialized parallel computing platform with a steep learning curve due to its Forth-native architecture. Backers face risks including potential hardware delivery delays, limited software ecosystem maturity, and the challenge of adopting a niche platform requiring significant programming expertise.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: Volatco is built by a team focused on transparent, low-level computing platforms for autonomous systems, with active documentation and community Discord support.

projects.ai_risks_en: Kubee is in late development with small-batch testing ongoing. Manufacturing challenges may arise during mass production, though the team expresses confidence in on-time delivery. Unforeseen issues may occur but will be addressed with transparent communication and prompt resolution.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: RobotsParis-based original brand creator of Kubee Robot, backed by the Ebaina/Yibaina user community.

ai_tiers.name: Make a pledge without a reward
ai_tiers.description: Back it because you believe in it. Support the project for no reward, just because it speaks to you.

ai_tiers.name: Available Reward
ai_tiers.description: Ships to anywhere in the world. Estimated delivery Sep 2026. 1 item included.

projects.name: aiSpine: Build Better Posture Habits with Smart AI Reminders
projects.blurb: A 7g wearable that senses posture, sends gentle vibration reminders, and helps you track daily posture habits through app-based AI
projects.hw_reason: Wearable posture sensor with vibration and app connectivity
projects.ai_intro_en: aiSpine is a lightweight 7g smart posture reminder wearable that combines motion sensing, gentle vibration reminders, app-based posture tracking, and AI-powered wellness insights to help users build better posture habits throughout the day.
ai_highlights_en: Ultra-lightweight 7g design for all-day comfort
High-precision 9-axis posture sensing with gentle vibration reminders
AI-powered app insights and up to 50 hours of battery life
ai_specs_en: AI Wellness Assistant, ChatGPT API chat module
Companion app for posture tracking and insights
projects.ai_risks_en: Hardware and software project risks include manufacturing delays, component sourcing, logistics, software refinement, regulatory compliance, and international fulfillment. The team has buffered the timeline by two months and is working with specialized partners to mitigate these challenges.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: AIH Technology Co., Ltd. is a health technology company focused on intelligent wellness devices, wearable sensing technologies, and AI-powered lifestyle management solutions.

projects.name: Thermal Tie Pro
projects.blurb: Thermal Tie Pro is an innovative wearable comfort technology designed specifically for professionals who work in cold environments.
projects.hw_reason: Wearable comfort technology for cold environments
projects.ai_intro_en: Thermal Tie Pro is a rechargeable heated necktie keeping professionals warm in cold environments without compromising business attire. It uses hidden carbon fiber heating elements powered by a USB-C battery with three heat settings and automatic shut-off safety features. The campaign funds prototype development and initial manufacturing.
ai_highlights_en: Discreet heated necktie combining professional style with wearable technology.
Features carbon fiber heating elements, USB-C battery, and 3 heat settings.
Includes overheat protection, auto shutoff, and removable washable electronics.
ai_specs_en: Wearable Technology / Business Apparel / Heated Clothing
Overheat protection, auto shutoff, insulated wiring
Removable electronics module, concealed battery pocket
projects.ai_risks_en: Campaign is at prototype stage with zero funding and backers. Production spans over a year, carrying risks of supply chain delays, safety certification hurdles, and battery compliance challenges before launch.
projects.ai_creator_bio_en: Matthew Maine, a Waynesboro, VA-based creator developing an innovative heated necktie accessory.
